data.title: Shanghai Seer flags strong revenue surge and narrower loss for first half of 2026
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Shanghai Seer Intelligent Technology Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:6106) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Shanghai Seer Intelligent Technology Co., Ltd., a Hong Kong-listed Chinese maker of intelligent robots and robot brains, is sharpening its focus on industrial automation at home and abroad as overseas markets become a key growth engine. The company’s emphasis on software-centric control systems and its push into export markets underscores its ambition to scale beyond China’s borders and capture rising demand for warehouse and factory automation.

The group expects first-half 2026 revenue of at least RMB252.6 million, up more than 60% year on year, and gross profit of at least RMB118.3 million, at least 65% higher than the prior period. Driven by strong sales of intelligent robots, control systems and surging overseas revenue, the company forecasts a narrowed net loss of no more than RMB40.5 million and a sharply reduced adjusted net loss, reflecting improving operational efficiency and tighter cost control ahead of detailed interim results due by end-August.

More about Shanghai Seer Intelligent Technology Co., Ltd. Class H

Shanghai Seer Intelligent Technology Co., Ltd. is a China-based provider of intelligent robots and so-called robot brains, focusing on automation solutions with a growing overseas presence. Listed in Hong Kong as a Class H share issuer, the company targets industrial and logistics customers seeking smart robotics and software-driven control systems to enhance operational efficiency and productivity.

Average Trading Volume: 645,920

Current Market Cap: HK$6.52B

data.title: China Railway Signal & Communication Wins Eight Major Rail Transit Contracts
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

An update from China Railway Signal & Communication ( (HK:3969) ) is now available.

China Railway Signal & Communication has secured eight significant contracts in China’s rail transit sector between May and June 2026, spanning both railway and urban metro projects. The wins include major system integration works on new mainline routes such as the Yining-Aksu Railway and the Mianyang-Suining-Neijiang Railway, as well as traction power upgrades and on-board CTCS-3 equipment for 350 km/h Fuxing trains.

In urban rail, the company will renew and upgrade signaling and video surveillance systems on key metro lines in Nanjing and Beijing, and supply the signal system for the first phase of Jinan Rail Transit Line 9. With total tender amounts in the hundreds of millions of renminbi, these awards reinforce the company’s leading role in China’s rail and metro signaling market and are likely to support future revenue and strengthen its competitive position in large-scale infrastructure projects.

More about China Railway Signal & Communication

China Railway Signal & Communication Corporation Limited is a Chinese rail transit technology company specializing in signaling, communication, train control and related system integration. It focuses on mainline railways and urban rail transit markets, providing equipment procurement, installation, commissioning and integrated solutions for high-speed, conventional and metro lines.

Average Trading Volume: 5,082,526

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$58.65B

Microware Group Limited has extended the long stop date for completing its placing of new shares under a general mandate, after agreeing a supplemental placing agreement with its placing agent. data.title: Hengrui Wins NMPA Nod to Move Cervical Cancer Combo to First-Line Use
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Jiangsu Hengrui Pharmaceuticals Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:1276) ) has shared an update.

Jiangsu Hengrui Pharmaceuticals and its subsidiary Suzhou Suncadia Biopharmaceuticals have received approval from China’s National Medical Products Administration to market their innovative drug combo, camrelizumab for injection with famitinib malate capsules. The regimen is now cleared as a first-line treatment for patients with recurrent or metastatic cervical cancer, upgrading its prior positioning from second-line to first-line therapy in advanced disease.

The approval strengthens Hengrui’s presence in oncology, particularly in targeted immunotherapy for gynecologic cancers, and underscores the company’s ability to move pipeline assets into earlier lines of care. This shift to first-line use could expand the eligible patient pool, enhance the commercial potential of camrelizumab and famitinib, and reinforce the firm’s competitive standing in China’s fast-growing market for innovative cancer treatments.

Unisound AI Technology Co., Ltd. has announced a board-approved plan to repurchase up to HK$0.1 billion worth of H shares on the open market within six months, under an existing mandate that permits buybacks of up to 4,510,988 H shares, or 10% of its issued H-share capital. data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions

Greentown China Holdings ( (HK:3900) ) has issued an announcement.

Greentown China Holdings has announced a major leadership reshuffle effective 23 July 2026, including the resignation of chairman and non-executive director Liu Chengyun, who also stepped down from leading the nomination, ESG and risk control committees and from his other roles in the group. Acting chief executive officer Geng Zhongqiang resigned from that role but will remain an executive director, with both executives stating they have no disagreement with the board.

The company has appointed Zhao Hui as executive director, chairman of the board, chief executive officer and authorised representative, as well as chairman of the nomination, ESG and risk control committees and a member of the remuneration committee, centralising key governance and operational responsibilities under his leadership. In addition, Jiang Feng has been appointed as an executive director and executive president, signaling a reconfigured top management team that may influence Greentown China’s strategic direction and operational oversight while aiming to ensure continuity and stability in corporate governance.

data.description:

Huaqin Technology Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:3296) ) has shared an announcement.

Huaqin Telecom, a wholly owned unit of Huaqin Co., Ltd., has increased its stake in Nexchip Semiconductor through an on-market purchase of 1,996,000 H shares in Hong Kong for about HK$65.8 million, lifting the group’s holding to roughly 10.91 percent. The acquisition, funded entirely with internal cash and based on prevailing market prices, expands Huaqin’s exposure to Nexchip’s 12-inch wafer foundry business, potentially deepening its integration into the semiconductor value chain.

Under Hong Kong listing rules, this latest purchase is aggregated with a series of Nexchip stake builds completed since August 2025, taking the combined transaction size over the 5 percent threshold but below 25 percent. As a result, the deal is classified as a discloseable transaction requiring reporting and public announcement but not shareholder approval, underscoring Huaqin’s ongoing strategic accumulation of Nexchip shares within regulatory limits.

More about Huaqin Technology Co., Ltd. Class H

International Business Settlement Holdings Limited has issued a supplemental announcement to its AGM circular detailing the proposed re-appointment of Rongcheng (Hong Kong) CPA Limited as auditor. The group’s operations, business scope and accounting environment are expected to remain broadly unchanged year-on-year, providing a basis for continuity in its audit arrangements and financial reporting.

The board, following the audit committee’s recommendation, plans to seek shareholder approval to re-appoint Rongcheng from the conclusion of the AGM until the next AGM and to authorise the board to fix its remuneration. The estimated audit fee for the year ending 31 March 2027 is set at HK$2 million to HK$2.2 million, which the board and audit committee deem fair and reasonable given Rongcheng’s familiarity with the group, expected audit scope and efficiency benefits for shareholders.

The company emphasises that the agreed audit terms reflect arm’s length negotiations and assume no material changes in operations, accounting policies or regulatory environment during the year. data.title: China Modern Dairy Signals Sharp Profit Turnaround for First Half of 2026
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

China Modern Dairy Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:1117) ) has provided an update.

China Modern Dairy Holdings Ltd. expects to post a profit before tax of at least RMB43 million for the first half of 2026, marking a sharp turnaround from a loss of about RMB972 million a year earlier. The improvement follows a reduced loss from changes in the fair value of dairy cows, helped by fewer culled cattle and higher selling prices, alongside stable gross margins on raw milk and higher gross profit.

The company also attributed the earnings recovery to ongoing cost-reduction and efficiency initiatives, including lower feed costs and tighter control of other expenses. Management is still finalizing the unaudited interim results, which are slated for release by the end of August 2026, and has cautioned shareholders and potential investors to remain prudent when trading the company’s securities pending the formal results announcement.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1117) stock is a Buy
with a HK$1.70 price target.

see the HK:1117 Stock Forecast page.

More about China Modern Dairy Holdings Ltd.

China Modern Dairy Holdings Ltd. is a dairy producer based in China, focusing on the breeding of dairy cows and the production and sale of raw milk. The group operates large-scale dairy farms and derives its revenue primarily from raw milk sales, positioning itself as a key player in China’s modern dairy farming sector.
